answer:
The python_exec tool gave us the Cartesian coordinates of the point with $r=3$ and $\theta = \frac{11\pi}{6}$, which is $\left(3 \cos \frac{11\pi}{6}, 3 \sin \frac{11\pi}{6}\right) = \left(3 \cdot \frac{\sqrt{3}}{2}, 3 \cdot \left(-\frac{1}{2}\right)\right) = \left(\frac{3\sqrt{3}}{2}, -\frac{3}{2}\right)$.
The python_exec tool also gave us the Cartesian coordinates of the point with $r=-3$ and $\theta = \frac{5\pi}{6}$, which is $\left(-3 \cos \frac{5\pi}{6}, -3 \sin \frac{5\pi}{6}\right) = \left(-3 \cdot \left(-\frac{\sqrt{3}}{2}\right), -3 \cdot \frac{1}{2}\right) = \left(\frac{3\sqrt{3}}{2}, -\frac{3}{2}\right)$.
The question asks for the equivalent point in the standard polar coordinate representation. We have $r = -3$, so we can write $r = 3$ and $\theta = \frac{5\pi}{6} + \pi = \frac{11\pi}{6}$.
Thus, the equivalent point is $\left(3, \frac{11\pi}{6}\right)$.

\boxed{\left(3, \frac{11\pi}{6}\right)}
